title: Mazars Comment: 2023 The Year Homework Will Pay Off
section_path: Mazars Comment: 2023 The Year Homework Will Pay Off
section_path_text: Mazars Comment: 2023 The Year Homework Will Pay Off
summary:
Main
By George Lagarias
The three things I would tell my clients this week:
With the debt ceiling behind us, we aren't really facing more event risks this year. But we are facing the longer-term, more predictable risks that come with tight credit conditions. Credit conditions are set to tighten in the next few months, and investors should not expect to see easier credit conditions until, possibly, 2025.

children.title: The year Homework Will Pay Off
section_path: The year Homework Will Pay Off
children.section_path_text: The year Homework Will Pay Off
children.summary: The debt ceiling drama-that-wasn't is squarely behind us. An eleventh-hour deal was signed, averting the wanton default of the biggest economy in the world, and the global risk-free asset, the US Dollar, marking the 79th straight time that the debt ceiling was successfully raised.
